helpful: Provisioned petition text + QP section — the whole case theory was readable without any fetch
Statpack per-Term paid/IFP split — the paid-class grant rate (~5.4%) is the right anchor here, not the blended ~2.5%
Snapshot's ProceedingsandOrder — the June 15 call-for-response after the government's waiver is the decisive signal and was right in the baseline
gaps: The committed statpack has no 'Segment base rate by salience band' section (the predict prompt tells cells to anchor on it) — signal composition had to be done by hand
No statpack cut for call-for-response petitions — CFR is the strongest common pre-grant docket signal after relists and has no corpus base rate
Dissents from denial of certiorari are not findable in the CourtListener opinion index, so vote-count signals (Hester/Rimlawi/Burnett) rest on the petition's own citations
notes: Forward cell, fresh same-day snapshot, clean inputs. One corpus query (148 GETs / ~38.7 MB) plus four MCP lookups stayed well under budget.
